atoms.source_snippet: Block processing service for scheduling transactions; SchedulerService owns SchedulerContract and processes BlockEvent messages.
atoms.semantic_claim: The old file wraps the scheduler contract monitor in a block-processing service loop.

destinations.evidence: Destination performs scheduled work inside the payload builder rather than through a standalone scheduling service loop.
Destination verifier enforces that due scheduled obligations are present or validly skipped.

atoms.equivalence_argument: The old service loop is not exact-preserved; its execution responsibility is absorbed by payload assembly and verification hooks.
atoms.negative_test_candidate: Disable payload scheduled obligation synthesis and confirm verifier catches a censorable due call, proving the service-loop responsibility moved to payload/verifier hooks.
atoms.notes: No destination service loop by this name or shape was found; behavior is distributed.

adversarial_reviews.reason: Standalone scheduling service loop is replaced by payload/verifier hooks.

summary: The old scheduling service loop is not exact-preserved; v2.2 distributes the responsibility into payload construction and block verification.
notes: Queue entry verified as file 420: A	crates/scheduling/src/service.rs.
